items.title: As the mercury drops in Canberra, cyclists are still braving the chill

children.content: Every year, winter sees many Canberrans hanging up their helmets, despite the city being considered Australia's "cycling capital." This year though, cyclist numbers have remained relatively steady, even as the mercury drops.

image.altText: A close up of the back wheel of a bike, with country road out of focus in the background.

items.title: ACT signs deal for CSIRO site, plans to build 3,000 homes

children.content: After being flagged for development over a decade ago, the ACT government has reached an agreement for the sale of land at a former CSIRO agriculture research site to construction of thousands new homes.

image.altText: A drone shot of green pastures

items.title: Aboriginal artist recreating 'oldest living weaving practice' with glass

children.content: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

gallery.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.

thumbnail.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: 'He gave us 10 extra years': Australian-first immune centre opens in Canberra

children.content: The Centre for Personalised Medicine at Canberra Hospital uses cutting-edge technology to identify the causes of complex immune conditions to tailor treatments for patients.

image.altText: An elderly man and his adult daughter smiling outside.

items.title: Canberra paramedics become patients every couple of days

children.content: ACT Ambulance Service recorded more than 190 occupational violence incidents last financial year.

image.altText: Mr Dutton smiles in his uniform, standing in front of the logo for the ACT Emergency Services Agency.

items.title: The story of Sidney Leonard Morse had been lost to history — until now

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

gallery.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

thumbnail.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

items.title: ADFA cadet facing 79 extra charges after hidden camera found in bathroom

children.content: Police allege Tain Stephens was living at the Australian Defence Force Academy in Canberra when he hid a camera inside a soap dispenser in a female bathroom to capture intimate recordings of women.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a t-shirt walks out of a court building.

items.title: Year 11 and 12 students should be forced to study maths, review says

children.content: A committee has found students in the ACT should study maths through to the end of year 12 to ensure they have better long-term outcomes, arguing a lack of maths understanding can exacerbate social inequality.

image.altText: A generic photo of a student writing on a pad.

items.title: UK police arrest 28-year-old man in Ann Widdecombe murder case

children.content: Police previously arrested, then later released, a 26-year-old man in relation to the alleged murder, after Ann Widdecombe's body was found in her residence on Thursday.

image.altText: An older lady speaks to the media.

items.title: Risks and rewards of playing diplomatic football in the Pacific

children.content: In a contest for Pacific influence against China, Australia's football diplomacy comes with risks and rewards.

image.altText: four men at the football field, two are wearing hoodies and two are wearing suits

items.title: Israeli court battle threatens to turn into constitutional crisis

children.content: A Trumpian clash between the Netanyahu government and Israel's most senior court is rapidly building into a major constitutional crisis.

image.altText: A graphic image depicting protests against Benjamin Netanyahu in Israel.

items.title: As Japan's population shrinks, 'urban bears' are moving in

children.content: When Araki Osamu first heard reports a bear had been seen in the streets of his home town, a 1-hour bullet train ride north of Tokyo, he dismissed it as "fake news".

gallery.altText: An illustration shows a bear poking its head into a Japanese building, maple trees and mountains in the distance

thumbnail.altText: An illustration shows a bear poking its head into a Japanese building, maple trees and mountains in the distance

items.title: In this friendship group there are 'no Jews, no Muslims, just one big family'

children.content: When a group of Jewish mates decided to support a newly arrived Muslim family, all of their lives would change. Together they have flipped expectations about religion and friendship.

image.altText: Five women and a man stand smiling outside a home with mother and adult son

items.title: Nosková downs 'ex-friend' Muchová to win all-Czech Wimbledon final

children.content: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.

image.altText: A young woman beams as she holds a giant sterling silver plate in front of a crowd.

items.title: Owner taken to court over $1m damage bill to 110yo cafe

children.content: The heritage-listed former Paragon Cafe in Katoomba was once considered a success story of Greek entrepreneurship, but has now landed in the NSW Land and Envicronment Court.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of the Paragon Cafe in Katoomba.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of the Paragon Cafe in Katoomba.

items.title: 'Mirror life' technology worries Doomsday Clock scientists. This is why

children.content: This year, scientists included a new potential threat in their decision to move the Doomsday Clock closer to midnight. So what is 'mirror life' and why are they concerned?

image.altText: Stylistic model of a section of a clock with the hands pointed towards midnight and sign saying 'It is 85 seconds to midnight'.

items.title: Can Australia avoid the next round of the global energy crisis?

children.content: As the conflict has dragged on, gas prices have soared and now threaten to up-end the economies of Europe and Asia.

image.altText: A LNG oil tanker at sea

items.title: From Heath Ledger protégé to abuse survivor, Grace Woodroofe perseveres

children.content: Heath Ledger launched her music career at just 17, but an emotionally abusive relationship with a fellow musician derailed it. A decade on, Grace Woodroofe is releasing the soundtrack to her survival.

items.title: How WWI helped shape what you call this deli meat

children.content: The words we use for a handful of classic Australian foods can tell a lot about where we come from.

items.title: The Invite welcomes you to one of the best films of 2026

children.content: Olivia Wilde's return to directing might take place almost entirely in a single room, but it unfolds all the relationship anxieties we prefer to keep hidden at home.

image.altText: The Invite header

children.content: Hidden for more than a century, a remarkable collection is offering a rare glimpse into the famed 1900s Discovery expedition to Antarctica.

items.title: Artist fighting Temu to reclaim her mosaic design

image.altText: A woman poses with her original mosaic work and its copy-cat that's been printed onto a rug sold on a drop ship website.

children.content: A South Australian artist is fighting drop-ship company Temu over its selling of her mosaic design on rugs. But how did the artwork get there in the first place?

items.title: The 100-million-year-old cold case and the aquatic dinosaur called Moria

children.content: Travis Enright is four months into investigating what he calls "a 100-million-year-old cold case" involving a 10-metre-long aquatic dinosaur called Moria.

image.altText: A man holding up a fossil bone.

items.title: NT government faces Federal Court challenge over compensation caps

children.content: The NT government's controversial laws capping compensation for prisoners unlawfully assaulted, strip searched or imprisoned are being challenged in the Federal Court.

children.content: Five of the people were killed when two glide bombs hit a crowded area in the northern city of ​Sumy, a frequent Russian target.

children.content: An Australian man has died in a Bali immigration detention centre while awaiting deportation.

items.title: Australian veterans say government will 'delay, deny until we all die'

children.content: It's been more than 50 years since Rifle Company Butterworth was deployed to protect Australia's air base overseas, yet they are still battling for recognition.

items.title: Teenage boy charged with murder of Victorian girl Layla Jeffery

children.content: The town of Donald in Victoria's west is in mourning, after remains believed to be missing 13-year-old Layla Jeffery were found by police on Thursday.

image.altText: A picture of a girl looking at the camera

items.title: Instagram pulls new AI feature after widespread backlash

children.content: The automatic feature, integrated into its Meta AI chatbot, let users edit generated images directly through sketches.

image.altText: A phone asking a number of prompts.

items.title: Sydney creatives revitalise underground music scene via online radio

children.content: Unlike 'terrestrial' stations, which transmit over AM or FM airwaves via radio towers, online radio is a more DIY approach.

thumbnail.altText: An image of a man sitting on a mustard colour couch.

items.title: Time to bite the bullet? Unpopular decision looms over $600m Aboriginal gallery

children.content: Adelaide's stalled Aboriginal cultural centre has a dubious future on a site with a troubled past. The Malinauskas government faces criticism whatever it chooses to do, writes state political reporter Thomas Kelsall.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a future use of the Lot Fourteen site.

items.title: Football homecoming brings 30yo tradition back to life

children.content: A special tribute match at Ensay's oval in regional Victoria resurrected a football tradition and brought generations of a Gippsland high country community together.

image.altText: A group of footy players pose in front of the score board after the game, smiling.

items.title: Mural celebrates pioneering star behind Australian country hits

children.content: A new mural in the country music capital celebrates the history of the musical McKean family but underneath the paint lies another family history, one which might not exist without the mural wall.
